items.title: HUD deadline: Apr. 14, 2026 to apply for HUD-VASH administrative fee funding
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-01 (Revised) on March 25, 2026, announcing $10 million in available funding for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH to support leasing HUD-VASH vouchers. Applications must be received by midnight April 14, 2026 (local time zone of the PHA).

items.title: HHS seeks comments (due Apr. 14, 2026) on updated LIHEAP RECS data match collection
items.summary: HHS/ACF’s Office of Community Services is proposing to reinstate (with changes) its collection of administrative LIHEAP beneficiary household data from states for the 2024–2025 RECS data match. The Federal Register notice says the changes would remove eight fields and reduce the estimated time per response from 24 to 16 hours, with comments due April 14, 2026.

items.title: Workforce Pell proposed rule: public comments due Apr. 8, 2026
items.summary: The Department of Education posted an NPRM titled “Accountability In Higher Education and Access Through Demand-Driven Workforce Pell,” with an NPRM comment due date of April 8, 2026. The proposal addresses Workforce Pell implementation and how Pell interacts with other grant aid.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit/veg cash benefit amounts; states must add frozen/canned/dried produce options by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says FY 2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts are $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes states must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SNAP FY 2026: maximum allotment is $298 (1 person) / $994 (4 people) in the 48 states + DC; minimum benefit is $24
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP COLA tables list FY 2026 maximum monthly SNAP allotments of $298 for a 1-person household and $994 for a 4-person household in the 48 states and DC. The tables also show a $24 minimum monthly allotment for 1–2 person households in the 48 states and DC, effective Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.

items.title: Social Security COLA for 2026: benefits rise 2.8%; SSI increases start Dec. 31, 2025
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 affecting about 75 million Americans, with nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries seeing the increase starting in January 2026. SSA said increased payments for nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients begin Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: CMS releases 2026–2027 Medicaid managed care rate development guide (rating periods starting July 1, 2026)
items.summary: A CMS informational bulletin dated March 12, 2026 says CMS released the 2026–2027 Medicaid Managed Care Rate Development Guide on Feb. 19, 2026. The bulletin says states use the guide for rating periods starting between July 1, 2026 and June 30, 2027 when preparing actuarial rate certifications.

items.title: IRS: Tax year 2026 inflation adjustments set max EITC at $8,231 (3+ qualifying children)
items.summary: IRS guidance on tax year 2026 inflation adjustments states the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) is $8,231 for qualifying taxpayers with three or more qualifying children. The same IRS materials list a 2026 standard deduction of $32,200 (married filing jointly), $16,100 (single/MFS), and $24,150 (head of household).

items.title: USDA updates SNAP Food Restriction Waivers list: Texas (Apr. 1), Florida (Apr. 20), Colorado (Apr. 30) and new long-dated waivers (e.g., Kansas Feb. 15, 2027; Nevada Feb. 1, 2028)
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists multiple state implementation dates, including Texas starting 04/01/26 (restricts sweetened drinks and candy), Florida starting 04/20/26 (restricts soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts), and Colorado starting 04/30/26 (restricts soft drinks). The same USDA list also shows recently approved waivers with later start dates, including Kansas (02/15/27) restricting candy and soft drinks and Nevada (02/01/28) restricting candy and sugar-sweetened beverages.

items.title: USDA: Secretary Rollins signs four new SNAP restriction waivers (Kansas, Nevada, Ohio, Wyoming) and previews retailer stocking standards final rule
items.summary: In a March 4, 2026 press release, USDA says Secretary Brooke Rollins signed four new SNAP restriction waivers for Kansas, Nevada, Ohio, and Wyoming. USDA also says a SNAP retailer “Stocking Standards” final rule is impending and would raise the minimum staple-food stocking requirements for stores that accept SNAP.

items.title: CMS: Medicaid/CHIP renewals in Oct. 2025—71% renewed (49% ex parte), 19% disenrolled (13% procedural), 10% pending
items.summary: CMS’s Feb. 24, 2026 All-State Medicaid and CHIP call slides report that for people due for renewal in October 2025, 71% had coverage renewed, including 49% renewed on an ex parte basis. The same CMS slides report 19% were disenrolled (13% for procedural reasons) and 10% were pending at the end of the month.

items.title: CMS enrollment snapshot: 76.8M enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP in Oct. 2025; down 260,000 from Sept. 2025
items.summary: CMS’s October 2025 Eligibility Operations and Enrollment Snapshot reports 76.8 million people enrolled in Medicaid and CHIP (69.5M Medicaid; 7.2M CHIP). CMS also reports total enrollment decreased by 260,000 (0.3%) from September 2025 to October 2025.
